title: Dark Software Factory — .factory/config.json
description: Per-repo factory configuration. Hooks resolve commands and paths ONLY from this validated file (a command allowlist), never by blind-evaluating arbitrary strings. The autonomous agent is fenced out of writing it (guard-scope).

testCommandRegex.description: regex matching the full-suite command (triggers record-green)

releaseVerbRegex.description: regex matching release verbs (gated by guard-release)

designDir.description: root of design-system sources (fenced to design-lead)

maxIterations.description: hard cap for a /factory-run lights-out loop

testCommand.description: explicit, unambiguous full-suite command the hooks INVOKE themselves when they must determine green deterministically (e.g. record-green with no exit-code evidence). Distinct from testCommandRegex, which only DETECTS that a suite ran. An allowlisted invoker, not a matcher.

enforcement.description: per-gate toggles for the HARD gates. Every gate defaults ON (true); set one to false to opt a single repo out of that gate deliberately and visibly, instead of the all-or-nothing plugin toggle. A session-local `.factory/state/paused` marker (human-set) relaxes every gate at once for one worktree.

requireGreenReceiptOnCommit.description: guard-commit: a tree-bound green receipt must exist to commit

requireTestsFirst.description: guard-commit: a feat/fix staging source must also stage a test (TDD)

conventionalCommitLint.description: guard-commit: the -m message must be a valid Conventional Commit

requireReleaseProof.description: guard-release: a release-gate proof must exist to release

enforceProjectDirScope.description: guard-scope/guard-bash-writes: writes resolving outside the project dir are denied

protectTrustRoots.description: guard-scope/guard-bash-writes: .factory/state, .factory/config.json, .factory/review are hook-managed and cannot be written directly

gates.description: the per-stage 'green' commands, run in order; empty stages are skipped

generators.description: generated-artifact sources → regenerate + diff at edit time (check-drift) and in CI

otel.description: optional, opt-in push-based OTEL metrics (collector-only, metrics-only MVP). Off by default: hooks never fork or touch the network unless enabled is exactly true.

enabled.description: turn on the emit calls in the hooks; no-op when false/absent

endpoint.description: OTLP/HTTP collector base URL; hooks POST to <endpoint>/v1/metrics

exporter.description: only OTLP/HTTP JSON is supported (zero-dep, hand-built payloads)
